message: Jake
Kristy
Günter
Hey, this is Snipes. It's been a while.
Have you been on a trip or something?
Something like that.
Is it about Miss Dern?
Welcome, Snipes.

There hasn't been any change since I last came here. It seems like you've been away for a while, but there's no dust or musty air to hint at that.
The sunlight that should be streaming in is blocked by closed curtains, and the fluorescent lights are illuminating the room.
It's a small room next to the examination room.
It's probably used for taking breaks or meeting patients' families.
He's still holding his usual cold pose. The way he looks at me through those thick lenses is just like a scholar observing a subject.
In a dark suit with a slim tie, the cheap pen sticking out of his chest pocket looks like something you could find anywhere.
He doesn't look rich at all.
His slightly receding hair is carefully slicked back. His broad forehead makes Günter look even more intellectual.
It's been a while, Snipes. I've been away at a conference for a bit, has anything changed?
I don't think there are any bad changes.
Oh, and my fiancée has come back.
Oh, that is quite interesting, isn't it?
I mentioned that the cause of her problems was the disappearance of her fiancé, right?
Yes, I've heard about it. I think extreme mental fatigue is likely the reason for her illness. Although there were factors before that.
What factors are you talking about? Guy's.
Isn't the fiancé's disappearance the root cause of everything?
It's not that simple. Whatever triggered the illness, without a seed, there can't be a sprout.
I see.
There are various factors, like congenital issues or childhood traumas; even trivial events can leave deep scars on the heart.
If you'd like to know more, I could give you a lecture when I have some free time.
By the way, she seems to have completely returned to normal since her fiancé came back.
Well, of course. At least she has been freed from one source of suffering.
Yes, that's true. However, I wondered if there is still a risk of her harming others.
Hmm.
If she had already committed a crime, that would be different, but it's true that having her fiancé around would help keep her in check.
But you know, Miss Dern is a person who changes quite often.
Isn't that just human nature?
Uh-huh.
Mm.
Anyway, I would like to meet her fiancé at least once.
Perhaps things will continue peacefully from here.
Assuming her fiancé stays with her, that is.
She seems to have returned to normal now, but can we really say she is cured?
I just want everything to go by without incident. As long as Gai is by her side, it should be okay.
But with Gai acting like that, I wonder if things will go well.
It’s one thing for university or public hospital staff, but can a mere private practitioner even be invited to conferences?
Is he actually a renowned and excellent doctor? Or,
is the case special enough to be reported at a conference?
If Gai comes back,
everything should return to normal.
This might be the last time I see her.
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
